comment: Exact lexical match; both are document subclasses representing papers.
Exact lexical match; both are document subclasses for reviews.
Exact lexical match; both are generic document classes.
Exact lexical match; both represent persons.
Near-exact lexical match differing by American/British spelling.
Exact lexical match; both represent a conference edition/event.
Lexically equivalent modulo separator; both denote conference sessions.
EDAS Topic aligns closely with EKAW Research_Topic as subject matter categories.
Both denote authors of papers; EDAS defines Author via relation to Paper.
Exact lexical match; both denote presenters.
Lexically equivalent modulo separator; both denote chairs of sessions.
TPCMember in EDAS corresponds closely to Program Committee member in EKAW.
Exact lexical match; both denote workshop events.
Lexically equivalent modulo separator; both represent social events.
Both denote the main conference meal/social dinner event.
Conference excursion and conference trip are closely related social event types.
Lexically equivalent modulo separator; both represent accepted papers.
Lexically equivalent modulo separator; both represent rejected papers.
AcademicEvent in EDAS is close in meaning to Scientific_Event in EKAW, though scope may differ slightly.
EDAS ConferenceEvent is a conference-related event and fits under the broader EKAW Event class.
Place and Location are closely corresponding spatial classes.
Both properties relate papers/events to topics.
Inverse-property mapping derived from hasTopic/coversTopic alignment.
Both properties relate events to locations/places.
Inverse-property mapping derived from hasLocation/heldIn alignment.
Both properties link documents/papers to their authors, though EDAS specializes to Paper-Author.
Both relate an author/person to a paper/document they authored; EDAS is more specialized.
Both relate papers to reviewers.
Inverse-side reviewer-paper relation corresponding to isReviewedBy/hasReviewer.
Both properties connect a paper to its presentation event, though directionality and scope differ.
